allies.rationale_text: Resilience captures the useful side of optimism by preserving energy for action without confusing morale with evidence.
allies.source_quote: The resilience fostered by an optimistic explanatory style

allies.rationale_text: Premortem gives optimism-bias-and-planning-fallacy a direct correction path by forcing downside imagination before commitment.
allies.source_quote: needs an outside-view check, a pre-mortem, or a reference class

allies.rationale_text: Problem-framing-and-reframing helps optimism-bias-and-planning-fallacy reopen the current story before conviction hardens into a bad plan.
allies.source_quote: might not be seeing their choices optimally

antagonists.rationale_text: System-2's slow, deliberate processing is the direct cognitive antidote to optimism-bias — the disciplined engagement of System 2 checks System 1's narrative-driven overoptimism and provides the outside-view discipline needed to correct planning fallacy.
antagonists.source_quote: The antagonist is the engagement of the algorithmic and reflective parts of System 2 (slow, contemplative thought), which provides the necessary discipline to check System 1's conclusions and ensure thinking aligns with reality.

antagonists.rationale_text: Intellectual-humility is a direct antagonist because it makes the planner worry they may not be seeing the choice optimally and forces real updating.
Systems-thinking counters optimism-bias-and-planning-fallacy by widening the frame beyond the internally coherent plan to the interdependencies that can break it.

structured_tensions.tension_text: Optimism-bias-and-planning-fallacy conflicts with growth-mindset when belief in improvement potential disguises the planning fallacy as reasonable ambition.
structured_tensions.source_quote: optimism-bias-and-planning-fallacy conflicts with growth-mindset (growth mindset) when belief in improvement potential disguises the planning fallacy as reasonable ambition.

structured_tensions.tension_text: Optimism-bias-and-planning-fallacy conflicts with commitment-bias when optimistic forecasts become anchors that commitment pressure prevents from being revised downward.
structured_tensions.source_quote: optimism-bias-and-planning-fallacy conflicts with commitment-bias (commitment bias) when optimistic forecasts become anchors that commitment pressure prevents from being revised downward.

curation_notes.summary: The raw source is strongest on the contrast between motivating optimism and the outside-view corrections supplied by humility and systems pressure, so the file preserves that conservative surface.
donor_drops: Did not preserve donor relation widening beyond the clearly named correction and resilience neighborhood.
